varieties.name: Yellow Rose Apple (Standard)
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree 7-12m, glossy narrow leaves, red new growth, white fluffy flowers
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow fruit 4-5cm, crisp, rose-petal flavored, hollow center with 1-4 seeds
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate; fruits for 6 months; bears in 3-4 years from seed
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical adaptation for humid subtropical climates. Tolerates humid summers. Susceptible to Myrtle Rust - requires treatment. Productive but not as vigorous as in tropics. Fragrant flowers attract bees. NOT invasive in Queensland gardens.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (NSW), limited Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Pink-Blushed Rose Apple
varieties.traits: Similar to standard but fruits develop pink blush on sun-exposed sides
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow with pink tinge, 4-5cm, crisp texture, strong rose fragrance
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, slightly less than yellow type
varieties.growing_notes: Performs well in Humid subtropical subtropical climate. Pink coloration enhanced by Queensland sunshine. Less commonly available.

varieties.name: Pink Wax Jambu
varieties.origin: Taiwan/Malaysia breeding, widely grown subtropical regions
varieties.traits: Small evergreen tree 3-10m, pink-red new growth, bell-shaped fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink-red waxy skin, white crisp flesh, 4-6cm, sweet and refreshing
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, 2-3 crops per year possible with management

varieties.name: Red Wax Jambu
varieties.traits: Dark red pear-shaped fruit, longer than Pink variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red waxy skin, crisp white flesh, highly ornamental, 5-7cm
varieties.growing_notes: Adapts well to humid subtropical summers. Striking appearance. Good for fresh eating. Requires full sun for best color development.

varieties.name: Green Giant
varieties.traits: Wide canopy, green fruit at maturity with rough surface texture
varieties.flesh_fruit: Green skin, 6-8cm, more seeds than pink/red varieties (2-3 seeds)
varieties.growing_notes: Less common in Australia. Suitable for humid subtropical climates climate. Unique appearance for niche markets.

varieties.name: Pearl (White)
varieties.traits: Pale greenish-white to white skin, highly prized
varieties.flesh_fruit: White skin and flesh, 5-6cm, very sweet, crisp texture

varieties.name: Black Pearl / Black Diamond
varieties.traits: Dark purple-red to nearly black fruit, premium variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark purple-red waxy skin, white flesh, 6-8cm, very sweet and juicy

varieties.name: Pink Water Apple
varieties.traits: Smaller tree 6-10m, evergreen, bell-shaped fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink-red skin, white flesh, 3-5cm, very juicy and watery
varieties.growing_notes: Marginally suitable for humid subtropical climates. Prefers more consistent tropical heat. Smaller fruit than S. samarangense. Good thirst quencher.

varieties.name: White Water Apple
varieties.traits: White to pale green fruit, compact tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: White skin, crisp but watery flesh, 3-4cm, mild sweet taste

varieties.name: Red Malay Apple
varieties.origin: Malaysia/Indonesia, ancient Polynesian introduction, 1000+ years cultivation
varieties.traits: Large tree 12-18m, evergreen, spectacular pink-red flowers, pear-shaped fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Shiny red-crimson skin, white crisp flesh, 5-8cm, mild sweet with rose-water hints
varieties.growing_notes: Marginal for humid subtropical climates - prefers more tropical conditions. Best in warmer northern suburbs. Ornamental value. Too tender for cold snaps.

varieties.name: White Malay Apple
varieties.traits: White to pale green fruit, large tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: White skin, crisp white flesh, 6-8cm, mild sweet flavor

varieties.name: Queensland Heritage Rose Apple
varieties.origin: Introduced Queensland circa 1896, over 125 years cultivation
varieties.traits: Seedling selections from early plantings, variable characteristics
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow to pink-blushed fruit, 4-5cm, authentic rose scent
varieties.growing_notes: Represents Queensland's subtropical adaptation over generations. Found in older gardens. More cold-hardy than recent introductions. Important genetic resource.
varieties.availability: Old homestead gardens, rare in commercial nurseries

varieties.name: Pink Chambakka (Standard Kerala) 🌏🌱 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Traditional tropical monsoon climates selection, widespread cultivation
varieties.traits: Bell-shaped pink fruit, vigorous tree, fast-growing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink to light red waxy skin, white flesh, 5-6cm, crisp texture
varieties.yield_notes: Very high, multiple fruiting per year in tropical monsoon climates climate
varieties.growing_notes: Perfectly adapted to Tropical monsoon tropical monsoon. Tolerates heavy rainfall and humidity. Essential fruit of tropical monsoon climates summers. Rich in fiber, calcium, magnesium, potassium, Vitamin C. Crunchy texture never sour.
varieties.availability: Widely available tropical monsoon climates nurseries, common backyard tree

varieties.name: Thailand Big Fruit (Introduced)
varieties.origin: Thailand breeding, introduced to tropical monsoon climates for commercial cultivation
varieties.traits: Large fruit size, vigorous growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Large fruits 6-8cm, pink-red skin, very sweet and delicious

varieties.name: Thub Thim Chan (Thai Introduction)
varieties.origin: Thailand cultivar, gaining popularity in India
varieties.traits: Long conical fruit shape, crimson flesh, extended production period
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crimson-pink skin and flesh, 6-7cm, very sweet, attractive appearance
varieties.yield_notes: High with long season (November-July in origin)

varieties.name: Indonesia Big Fruit (Perfume Wax Apple)
varieties.traits: Large fruit with special aroma, 6-9cm
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark pink-red skin, white crisp flesh, fragrant, 150-200g per fruit
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in Tropical monsoon monsoon climate. Distinctive perfume-like aroma. Susceptible to chilling (not an issue in tropical monsoon climates). Premium quality for fresh market.
varieties.availability: Limited in tropical monsoon climates, specialty importers

varieties.name: Vietnam White
varieties.traits: Pale white-green fruit, compact habit
varieties.flesh_fruit: White skin, sweet white flesh, 5-6cm

varieties.name: Big Red Malaysia
varieties.traits: Deep red coloration, large fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright red skin, white flesh, 6-8cm, juicy and sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for tropical monsoon climates climate. Striking appearance. Good commercial potential.

varieties.name: Indian Rose Apple (Gulab Jamun) 🌏🏆 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Native to India, cultivated throughout tropical monsoon climates and South India for centuries
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree, glossy leaves, white fluffy flowers, yellow fruit with rose fragrance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow fruit 4-5cm, crisp mealy flesh, authentic rose petal flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, fruits for extended period in tropical monsoon climates climate
varieties.growing_notes: Historically significant - India anciently called Jambudvipa (Rose-apple-land). Traditional brain and liver tonic in Ayurveda. Used to prepare rose water and wine. Thrives in Tropical monsoon humid monsoon climate. Important cultural and medicinal plant. Blooms in January, fruits ripen March-April and June-July in South India.
varieties.availability: Traditional gardens throughout tropical monsoon climates, ICAR-KVK stations, heritage nurseries

varieties.name: Pink Water Apple
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, naturalized in humid Indian regions
varieties.traits: Smaller tree, evergreen, bell-shaped fruit, prefers stream banks
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink-red skin, very watery white flesh, 3-5cm, astringent when unripe
varieties.growing_notes: Well-adapted to Tropical monsoon monsoon rains and humidity. Often planted along water bodies. Good for fresh eating and salads. Less sweet than S. samarangense but refreshing.
varieties.availability: Available in Andhra Pradesh, tropical monsoon climates, Maharashtra nurseries

varieties.name: Red Malay Apple
varieties.traits: Large tree 12-18m, spectacular pink-red flowers, pear-shaped fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Shiny deep red skin, white crisp flesh, 5-8cm, mild sweet flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for Tropical monsoon tropical monsoon climate. Requires humid climate with 152cm+ annual rainfall. Most beautiful of all Syzygium species. Used traditionally in tropical monsoon climates for medicines (bark treats stomach issues). Often stewed with other fruits.
varieties.availability: Limited in tropical monsoon climates, more common in Java, Sumatra, Peninsular Malaysia

varieties.name: Thiruvalla Chambakka (Local Landrace) 🌏🏆 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates region traditional selection, generations of cultivation
varieties.traits: Local adaptation to tropical monsoon climates microclimate, variable fruit colors
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rose to red fruit, 4-6cm, adapted to local soil and climate
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable in tropical monsoon climates conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Represents generations of local selection and adaptation. Found in traditional homesteads. Important genetic diversity. Well-suited to Tropical monsoon specific climate patterns and soil types. Cultural significance in local festivals and celebrations.
varieties.availability: Local gardens, traditional homesteads in tropical monsoon climates area

varieties.name: Kottayam Heritage Chambakka 🌏🏆 INDIGENOUS
varieties.traits: Regional selection, adapted to central tropical monsoon climates conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink-red fruit, traditional characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Good in central tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Palakkad Seedling Selection 🌏🌱 INDIGENOUS
varieties.traits: Diverse characteristics from natural seedling variation
varieties.flesh_fruit: Variable colors and sizes, true-to-type from seed
varieties.yield_notes: Variable but adapted to local conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Cheerakuzhy Nurseries Group (major tropical monsoon climates supplier) specializes in rose water apple. Natural seedling variation provides genetic diversity and local adaptation. Can be grown successfully from seed.
